page: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 architecture, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the m9g.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory and compression capabilities. It achieves a top-tier rating in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.42 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 17.14 GB/sec. PassMark memory testing places the instance in the top tier with a score of 4552.85, supported by a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 2282.43 GB/sec. Multi-threaded performance is also notable, with bzip3 decompression reaching 257.5 GB/sec and PassMark database operations scoring 29240.13. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance delivers top-tier text generation speeds of 376.71 tokens/sec on a small 135M model and strong performance of 30.2 tokens/sec on a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARMv9 architecture offers a high-density resource profile, delivering strong memory bandwidth and compression performance relative to its general-purpose hardware class. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs limits its utility for heavy storage-bound databases or intensive deep learning training. The instance is highly suited for workloads such as static web serving, database operations, text processing, and lightweight LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor and powered by 16 dedicated ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers 8 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. In performance benchmarks, the m9g.4xlarge excels in memory operations and compression, achieving top-tier scores in PassMark memory testing and single-threaded gzip compression. It also demonstrates strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and database operations. While the absence of local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff for disk-heavy applications, its high memory bandwidth and efficient ARM architecture make it highly suitable for static web serving, database workloads, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is an ARMv9-based general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Neoverse-V3 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.4xlarge is a general-purpose ARMv9 instance with 16 dedicated cores, 64 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARMv9-powered general-purpose instance delivering exceptional memory bandwidth, high-speed compression, and balanced compute resources for diverse enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression performance
Features 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for static web serving, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference
